name: Kotonjata (Christmas Candy)
recipeInstructions:
1. Select an even number of evenly colored quinces (not spotted). I use 13 large quinces.
2. Wash and peel the quinces as you would apples.
3. Grate the flesh using the largest holes.
4. Place all the grated fruit in a heavy pot with the juice from 2 lemons and 2 cups of water.
5. Cook over the lowest flame for at least an hour, or an hour and a half, until the quince is soft.
6. Remove from heat and measure the cooked quince by cup. Return to the pot.
7. Add an equal amount of quince and sugar to the pot. Stir well until fully blended.
8. Cook on medium heat, watching carefully that it doesn't scorch. Reduce heat to the lowest setting for another hour, stirring frequently.
9. After the first half hour, add 1 cup of strained lemon juice.
10. Test by placing a teaspoonful on a small plate. If it holds its shape and doesn't run, it's ready.
11. Pour the mixture onto platters or into a Pyrex baking dish about 1/4 inch thick.
12. Let dry for 3 days, turning frequently.
13. Cut into smaller pieces to help turn easier.
14. When hardened, wrap and refrigerate until used.
